cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out which of the two statements is sarcastic. The options are (A) and (B). Let me start by understanding what sarcasm is. Sarcasm is when someone says the opposite of what they really mean, often with a tone of mock or scorn. So the key here is to identify which statement is using irony or mock surprise. In statement (A), the team they're losing to has a terrible record, which would be unexpected because a team with a bad record isn't expected to win. The second part of (A) is "I for one am shocked." If you're shocked that a team with a terrible record is winning, that's actually plausible.
